name: African Elephant
hints: I am one of the largest land animals on Earth.
I use a long flexible nose to grab food and water.
My huge ears are shaped a bit like the continent I’m named after.
diet: Mainly grasses, leaves, bark, and fruit from trees and shrubs.
funFact: African elephants can communicate using low-frequency sounds that travel several kilometers through the ground.

name: Emperor Penguin
hints: I look like I’m wearing a black and white tuxedo.
I cannot fly, but I slide and swim very well on ice and in water.
I keep my egg warm by balancing it on my feet in the freezing cold.
diet: Primarily small fish, krill, and squid caught in icy ocean waters.
funFact: Emperor penguins can dive over 500 meters deep and hold their breath for more than 20 minutes.

name: Red Panda
hints: I have a long fluffy tail with rings and a masked face.
I spend much of my time in trees, especially in cool mountain forests.
Although my name says otherwise, I am not a bear but have my own special family.
diet: Mostly young bamboo leaves and shoots, plus fruits, berries, and the occasional egg or small insect.
funFact: Red pandas use their big, bushy tails like blankets to stay warm while they sleep in the trees.
